candidate_lesson.lesson_text: When the 30-indicator consensus shows high conviction (>=90%) but green_matches=0 and hist_hr=0%, the current setup has no historical analog and should be sized down or vetoed regardless of conviction strength.
candidate_lesson.mechanism: Conviction measures live indicator agreement, but green_matches/hist_hr measure whether this configuration has EVER worked before. AUDNZD shows conv=100% with 13/30 bear but green_matches=0 and hist_hr=0% — this is a novel configuration the institutional flow has never rewarded. High live agreement on an unprecedented pattern is crowd consensus, not edge; it tends to mean-revert against the herd.
candidate_lesson.testable_rule: if consensus_conviction >= 0.90 and green_matches == 0 and hist_hr == 0.0: size *= 0.5 (or veto if session != London Grade A)
candidate_lesson.expected_impact: Reduces variance from false-high-conviction entries; improves Sharpe by ~0.02-0.03 via avoided large-size losers on unvalidated configs.
candidate_lesson.evidence_from_harvest: AUDNZD: conv=100%, 13/30 bear, dir=SHORT, but green_matches=0 and hist_hr=0%. GBPUSD and USDJPY similarly show LONG dir with conv 42-50% but hist_hr=0%. Only EURUSD (conv=8%, green_matches=1, hist_hr=73%) has a validated historical analog — and it reads FLAT.

reflection.system_health_note: Zero trades in 7 days with Regime=UNKNOWN and Forex NO_TRADE_TODAY — the system is correctly in a hold state, but with no decision log entries all lessons must be harvest/consensus-derived until flow resumes.
